english: of (the house), (he comes) from without (the village), frq.; *ṗí-log* (phi log) W. id.; *cág-ri ṗi-log la* (lcags ri phi log la), outside before the (garden) wall. 2. mystic: ydon bgegs ṗyi-rol-tu odzin-pa (gdon bgegs phyi rol tu 'dzin pa), to believe goblins and demons to be really existing in the outer world Mil. — ṗyi-sa (phyi sa), excrements S.g.; the supposed food of certain demons Thgy. — ṗyi-lha (phyi lha)? IV. ṗyi-la (phyi la), on account of, v. ṗyir (phyir).
Cs.; a blow with the side of the hand.
to clean, to cleanse Dzl. and elsewh.; byád-kyi ṗyi-bdár bsól-nas kyaṅ (byad kyi phyi bdar bsol nas kyang) though you do not wash your face Mil.
a kind of ornament, similar to paṅ (pang).
S.g., oṗyí-ba ('phyi ba) Lt., 1. the large marmot of the highlands of Asia, Arctomys Boibak. -- 2. v. obyi-ba ('byi ba).
I. col. *ă-ṗyi* (a phyi), *ă-ṗi* (a phi), grand-mother, Cs. II. v. ṗyi (phyi) II.

Pur. *ṗyiṅ-pa* (phying pa); Ld., Lh. *ṗiṅ-pa* (phing pa), elsewh. čiṅ-pa (ching pa), felt, odéd-pa ('ded pa), to make felt, to mill, to full Sch.; ṗyiṅ-gúr (phying gur), felt-tent, a Tartar hut; ṗyiṅ-stán (phying stan), felt-carpet, felt-covering; ṗyiṅ-déb (phying deb) Sch.: a wrapper or cover made of felt.
= ṗyi (phyi), after, following; ṗyid-nyin (phyid nyin), the day after to-morrow, Cs.

I. (v. ṗyi (phyi) ni f.) to retard, prolong, maintain, with tse (tshe): to maintain one's life, to earn a livelihood, W. e.g. *gár-ra čó-te* (mgar ra bco te) or *čós-si náṅ-ne tse ṗid-če* (chos si nang ne tshe phyid ce), to maintain one's self as a smith, or by religion, (being a Lama). -- II. to freeze, *káṅ-pa ṗid-soṅ* (rkang pa phyid song), the foot is frozen, suffering from chilblains; *mig ṗid soṅ* (mig phyid song), the eyes are inflamed, snow-blind, W. (C. *čë* (che)). -- III. v. oṗyid ('phyid); byid (byid).

for ṗyi (phyi), in certain phrases: 1. ṗyin-čád (phyin chad), -čád (-chad), later, afterwards, ṗyin-čád sdom (phyin chad sdom), bound over for the time to come, e.g. not to do a thing again; da ṗyin-čád (da phyin chad), from the present moment, from henceforth, frq.; odí ṗyin ('di phyin), id.; de ṗyin-čád (de phyin chad), rarely de ṗyin-nas (de phyin nas), Tar. 57, 2 since, since that time, ever since. -- 2. outside, ṗyin rtsig-pa méd-de (phyin rtsig pa med de) as there was no wall outside Glr.; ṗyin-dgrá (phyin dgra) a foreign enemy Glr.; ṗyin-las (phyin las) outward business, foreign affairs Dzl.

anything wrong, incorrect, deceptive, fallacious; perversity; ṗyin-či-lóg-gis bslád-de (phyin ci log gis bslad de) corrupt, depraved by perversity Dzl.; ṗyin-či ma lóg-pa (phyin ci ma log pa) it is infallible (of a spell), synon. to bdén-pa (bden pa); ltá-ba ṗyin-či ma lóg-pa (lta ba phyin ci ma log pa) correct view, opinion Pth.; ṗyin-či-lóg-tu stón-pa (phyin ci log tu ston pa) to teach a false doctrine; blo ṗyin-či ma lóg-par (blo phyin ci ma log par), with a never erring mind Mil.

I. B., C. *čín-pa* (chin pa), Sp. *ṗín-pa* (phin pa), little used in W.: 1. to come, to get to, advance, arrive; lam ṗyed tsám-du (lam phyed tsam du), having got about midway, Dzl.; der ṗyin-pa daṅ (der phyin pa dang), frq.; ču ṗrág-pa tsám-du ṗyin-to (chu phrag pa tsam du phyin to), the water reached up to his shoulders, Dzl.; *ṗin-na* (phin na) Sp., is he arrived? sbyín-pai ṗá-rol ṗyin (sbyin pa'i pha rol phyin), that goes farther than alms-giving, surpasses it, Glr.: dpag-tsád lṅar ṗyin-pa (dpag tshad lngar phyin pa), to be five miles in length, Dzl. -- 2. to go, to proceed, sṅón-la ṗyin-pa (sngon la phyin pa), Pth.; ma ṗyin-par sleb (ma phyin par sleb), without going from the place, he arrives at ... Mil.; bud-méd dëi rtsar ma ṗyin (bud med de'i rtsar ma phyin), he did not go to the woman (euphemist.) Glr.; stab-stob-du naṅ-du ṗyin-te (stab stob du nang du phyin te), he went in, ran in, in a great hurry. (Probably the word is cog. to ṗyi (phyi), and therefore = obyúṅ-ba ('byung ba), otón-pa ('thon pa).) -- II. v. obyín-pa ('byin pa).

prop. the termin. of ṗyi (phyi): I. 1. adv. back, towards the back, behind; ṗyir oóṅ-ba (phyir 'ong ba), to come back, to return Dzl. and elsewh., frq.; also used in a special sense rel. to re-birth lan-yčig ṗyir oóṅ-ba (lan gcig phyir 'ong ba), ṗyir mi oóṅ-ba (phyir mi 'ong ba) v. obrás-bu ('bras bu) (bżi) (bzhi); ṗyir ogró-ba (phyir 'gro ba), ṗyir odóṅ-ba (phyir 'dong ba) etc., id.; ṗyir dúg-pa (phyir dug pa), to remain behind, at home, Dzl.; ṗyir jóg-pa (phyir 'jog pa), to leave behind, at home, to lay aside, to lay up, Dzl.; again (rursus), ṗyir láṅ-ba (phyir lang ba), to get up again, after having fallen; ṗyir ldóg-pa (phyir ldog pa), lóg-pa (log pa), to come back again, to return; ṗyir ldóg-pai lam (phyir ldog pa'i lam), the way back, the return, Dzl.; ṗyir mi ldóg-pa (phyir mi ldog pa), the not <
